product: External MCP servers are in Beta
tagline: Users can now connect Databricks to external Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers.
what_it_does: Users can now connect Databricks to external Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. This allows agents to access tools outside of Databricks. MCP is an open source standard that connects AI agents to tools, resources, prompts, and other contextual information.
why_it_matters: On Databricks, Unity AI Gateway, the enterprise control plane, governs access and monitors activity across MCP servers and LLM endpoints.
plain_summary: Users can now connect Databricks to external Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. This allows agents to access tools outside of Databricks.

capabilities.title: Connect to MCP servers hosted
capabilities.desc: Connect to MCP servers hosted outside Databricks using managed connections, and register them as Unity Catalog securables to…

capabilities.title: Host your own MCP server
capabilities.desc: Host your own MCP server as a Databricks app.

key_takeaway: Users can now connect Databricks to external Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers.
